title: Pedigo P-2040 Blanket Warmer - Not Heating
description: Troubleshooting a Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer that powers on but does not heat, heats poorly, or fails to maintain set temperature.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on a blanket warmer that is not maintaining temperature for patient care. Remove blankets from the warmer if they feel cold, overheated, damp, or unsafe for patient use. If warming is needed, direct staff to use another approved blanket warmer. Do not troubleshoot the unit while it is being actively used for patient care. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without using blankets from a questionable warmer. If this resolves the immediate clinical need, stop and document the equipment issue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Heating Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the unit is completely not heating or just not reaching the expected temperature. Check the displayed temperature, set temperature, and actual cabinet warmth. Ask staff what they observed: unit cold, blankets not warm enough, temperature dropping, long warm-up time, alarm or error displayed, or heat works intermittently. Allow enough time for warm-up if the unit was just powered on. Expected outcome: You confirm whether this is a true no-heat condition or a slow/recovery heating complaint. If the warmer begins heating normally after adequate warm-up time, return it to service and document the finding.

steps.title: Check Power at the Outlet
steps.instructions: A blanket warmer may appear powered in some cases but still have supply issues, especially if the outlet, cord, or plug is compromised. Verify the power cord is fully plugged into a hospital-grade outlet. Check whether the outlet is controlled by a wall switch or GFCI. Test the outlet with an approved outlet tester or known-good device. Inspect for loose fit, damaged receptacle, tripped breaker, or power strip use. Expected outcome: The warmer has stable power from an approved outlet. If outlet power is the issue, move the unit to a verified outlet or notify Facilities as appropriate, then stop.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and Plug
steps.instructions: A damaged cord can cause intermittent power or unsafe operation. Inspect the full length of the cord for cuts, crushed insulation, exposed conductors, burn marks, bent blades, or loose strain relief. Check the plug for heat damage or discoloration. Gently flex the cord near the plug and cabinet while observing for power interruption. Expected outcome: Cord and plug are intact and safe. If cord damage, heat damage, or intermittent power is found, remove the warmer from service and send for repair.

steps.title: Confirm the Unit Is Turned On and Set Correctly
steps.instructions: Incorrect settings can mimic a heating failure. Verify the main power switch is on. Check that the temperature control is enabled and set within the facility-approved blanket warming range. Confirm staff did not turn the setpoint down or place the warmer in a low-temperature state. Look for any display, indicator light, or heating status light that shows whether the unit is calling for heat. Expected outcome: The warmer is powered, enabled, and requesting heat. If the setting was incorrect and the cabinet begins warming normally, document the correction and return the device to service.

steps.title: Check Door Closure and Gasket Condition
steps.instructions: If the door is not sealing, heat may escape faster than the unit can recover. Inspect the door for full closure. Check the gasket for tears, gaps, looseness, compression damage, or debris. Verify hinges and latch hardware are secure. Confirm blankets or packaging are not blocking the door from sealing. Expected outcome: The cabinet door seals properly and holds heat. If the door was blocked or not fully closed and the unit recovers temperature, stop and document the cause.

steps.title: Check Cabinet Loading
steps.instructions: Overloading can slow heat recovery and make the warmer appear weak. Inspect the cabinet load. Remove excessive blankets if the unit is packed tightly. Make sure blankets are not blocking vents, airflow openings, or internal circulation paths. Avoid placing cold bulk loads into the warmer and expecting immediate recovery. Expected outcome: Air can circulate inside the cabinet and the warmer can recover temperature normally. If overloading caused poor heating, educate staff, allow recovery, and document the finding.

steps.title: Look for Heating Indicators, Alarms, or Abnormal Signs
steps.instructions: The unit may provide clues even without a detailed error code. Observe the display and indicator lights. Listen for unusual clicking, buzzing, or fan noise if applicable. Check for abnormal smell, smoke, excessive heat at the plug, or cabinet surfaces that feel unsafe. Note whether the unit is completely cold or slightly warm. Expected outcome: You identify whether the unit is attempting to heat or showing signs of a fault. If there is burning smell, smoke, overheating, or electrical damage, unplug the unit if safe, remove it from service, and escalate immediately.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Warmer
steps.instructions: A simple control reset may clear a temporary control issue. Turn the warmer off. Unplug it from the outlet. Wait at least 30 seconds. Plug it back into a verified outlet and power it on. Set the proper temperature and observe whether heating begins. Expected outcome: The warmer resumes normal heating. If the unit heats normally after the reset, monitor temperature recovery and document the reset.

steps.title: Verify Temperature Recovery
steps.instructions: Do not return the unit based only on lights or display activity. Allow the warmer time to heat. Compare the displayed cabinet temperature to an independent temperature reading if your department procedure allows it. Confirm the unit approaches and maintains the set temperature. Observe for overshoot, no heat, slow heat, or temperature dropping after warm-up. Expected outcome: The warmer reaches and maintains a safe, stable operating temperature. If temperature does not rise or cannot be verified, remove the unit from service.

steps.title: Check for Repeat Failure
steps.instructions: Intermittent no-heat complaints should not be dismissed just because the unit works during a quick check. Ask staff if the issue happens after hours of use, after the door is opened, after loading blankets, or after moving the unit. Inspect for loose cord connection, damaged plug, unstable outlet, or signs of intermittent control operation. Perform an extended observation if the complaint is repeatable. Expected outcome: Either the issue is confirmed or the device passes a reasonable operational check. If the failure repeats or cannot be confidently cleared, remove the warmer from service for bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer was powered on but blankets were not getting warm.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet power and settings were correct, but cabinet temperature did not rise during observation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the blanket warmer from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation for suspected internal heating/control failure.

helpfulDetails: Whether cabinet was cold, slightly warm, or overheating
Any alarm, smell, unusual sound, or hot plug

title: Pedigo P-2040 Blanket Warmer - Temperature Too Low
description: Troubleshooting a Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer that is not reaching set temperature or is warming blankets poorly.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on blankets from a warmer that may not be maintaining the expected temperature. Remove any questionable blankets from clinical use until temperature performance is verified. Notify staff that the warmer may not be heating properly. Use blankets from another approved warmer if patient warming is needed. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without relying on a blanket warmer with uncertain temperature performance.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Temperature Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the unit is actually too cold or whether the complaint is based on user perception. Check the displayed temperature, set temperature, and actual cabinet condition. Ask staff when the issue was noticed: after restocking, after the door was left open, after power loss, or after relocation. Expected outcome: The issue is confirmed as either a true low-temperature condition or a normal recovery delay. If the warmer is recovering normally after being loaded or opened frequently, allow time to stabilize and stop.

steps.title: Check That the Unit Has Power
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer is powered and not partially energized. Confirm the power switch is on. Check that the display or indicator lights are active. Verify the power cord is fully seated and not damaged. Test the outlet or plug the warmer into a known-good hospital-grade outlet if safe to do so. Expected outcome: The unit powers normally and begins heating. If power was the issue and the warmer heats normally afterward, return it to service after temperature verification and stop.

steps.title: Check the Temperature Setting
steps.instructions: Confirm the set temperature has not been lowered. Review the current setpoint. Compare it with the department’s approved blanket warming temperature range. Do not raise the setpoint beyond facility policy or manufacturer recommendations. Expected outcome: The warmer is set appropriately for clinical use. If the setpoint was incorrect, correct it, allow the cabinet to stabilize, verify temperature, document the finding, and stop.

steps.title: Check for Recent Loading or Overloading
steps.instructions: A blanket warmer may read low or feel cool after being heavily restocked. Ask whether cold blankets were recently added. Check whether the cabinet is packed too tightly. Make sure air can circulate around the contents. Remove excess blankets if airflow is blocked. Expected outcome: Air circulation improves and the unit begins recovering toward set temperature. If overloading caused the issue, educate the department, allow recovery time, verify temperature, and stop.

steps.title: Check Door Closure and Gasket Condition
steps.instructions: A door that does not seal properly can prevent the cabinet from reaching temperature. Confirm the door closes completely. Inspect the gasket for gaps, tears, compression damage, or debris. Check that blankets are not blocking the door from sealing. Look for a loose handle, hinge issue, or misaligned door. Expected outcome: The door seals fully and warm air stays inside the cabinet. If the door was blocked or not fully closed, correct the issue, allow the warmer to recover, verify temperature, and stop.

steps.title: Check Ambient Conditions and Placement
steps.instructions: Poor placement can affect warming performance. Confirm the warmer is not located directly under heavy airflow from HVAC vents. Check that the unit is not against walls or objects that restrict ventilation. Make sure the warmer is in a normal indoor clinical environment. Expected outcome: The warmer has proper airflow and is not being cooled by the room environment. If placement was the issue, relocate or correct the airflow concern, then verify temperature recovery.

steps.title: Listen for Abnormal Operation
steps.instructions: Check for signs that the heating system or internal air circulation may not be functioning normally. Listen for unusual sounds, buzzing, clicking, or silence when the unit should be heating. Check for unusual heat, burning smell, or electrical odor. Do not continue troubleshooting if overheating, odor, smoke, or damaged wiring is present. Expected outcome: No unsafe sounds, smells, or heat conditions are present. If there is an electrical smell, unusual heat, smoke, or visible damage, remove the warmer from service immediately and send it for repair.

steps.title: Verify Actual Cabinet Temperature
steps.instructions: Do not rely only on touch. Use a calibrated thermometer or approved temperature verification device. Place it inside the cabinet according to your department’s normal temperature-check process. Allow enough time for the reading to stabilize. Compare the measured temperature to the display and setpoint. Expected outcome: Actual cabinet temperature matches the expected range. If the measured temperature is acceptable, explain findings to staff, document the verification, and return the warmer to service.

steps.title: Check for Temperature Display Mismatch
steps.instructions: The unit may be heating, but the display or sensing may be inaccurate. Compare displayed temperature to the independent measured temperature. Look for a large difference between the display and measured cabinet temperature. Check whether the temperature continues rising, remains flat, or drops. Expected outcome: Displayed and measured temperatures are reasonably consistent. If the cabinet is warm but the display reads low, suspect a sensing, display, or control issue and escalate for bench evaluation.

steps.title: Perform a Controlled Recovery Check
steps.instructions: If no simple cause is found, observe whether the warmer can recover. Close the door. Leave the warmer powered on and undisturbed. Allow reasonable stabilization time. Recheck actual temperature. Expected outcome: The warmer steadily rises toward set temperature and maintains temperature. If the temperature does not rise, rises very slowly, or cannot maintain temperature, remove it from service.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported that the Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer was not getting warm enough and blankets felt cool during use.
CCR.Cause: Verified the unit had power, but the cabinet temperature remained below the setpoint after door closure, loading, outlet, and placement checks.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the blanket warmer from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation for suspected heating or temperature control failure.

helpfulDetails: Environmental factors such as HVAC airflow or restricted ventilation

title: Pedigo P-2040 Blanket Warmer - Temperature Too High
description: Troubleshooting a Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer that is overheating, reading high temperature, or warming above the expected set range.

steps.title: 1. Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not continue using blankets or linens from a warmer suspected of overheating. Remove the Pedigo P-2040 from active clinical use if the cabinet temperature is above the expected range, if blankets feel excessively hot, or if staff report a temperature alarm or overheating concern. Remove heated contents if safe to do so. Check whether any patient-contact items may have been overheated. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without risk of thermal injury from overheated blankets or linens. Why it matters: Blanket warmers can cause patient burns if contents are overheated or applied directly to sensitive skin.

steps.title: 2. Verify the Reported Temperature Condition
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the unit is actually overheating or if the concern is based on display behavior. Check the displayed temperature. Compare it to the set temperature. Ask staff what they observed, including high temperature display, alarm condition, blankets too hot to touch, cabinet running continuously, or unusual smell or heat from the unit. Expected outcome: You confirm whether the issue is a true high-temperature condition, a display concern, or a user-reported comfort issue. If the displayed temperature is normal and blankets are not excessively hot, document the finding and continue monitoring if needed.

steps.title: 3. Remove the Unit From Service if Temperature Is Unsafe
steps.instructions: Do not try to “watch it for a while” in patient care areas if the warmer is already above a safe operating range. If the cabinet temperature is significantly above the setpoint, unplug the warmer. Label it Out of Service. Allow the cabinet to cool before further inspection. Expected outcome: The overheating condition is stopped and the unit is controlled before further use.

steps.title: 4. Check the Temperature Setpoint
steps.instructions: Verify that the set temperature was not accidentally adjusted too high. Review the temperature setting on the control panel. Confirm it matches the facility’s approved blanket warmer temperature policy. Adjust only within approved clinical limits. Expected outcome: If the setpoint was too high, correcting it should allow the warmer to stabilize at the proper temperature. If this resolves the issue, return to service only after confirming stable temperature operation.

steps.title: 5. Check for Blocked Airflow Inside the Cabinet
steps.instructions: Overloading the cabinet can trap heat and cause uneven or elevated temperatures. Inspect the cabinet contents. Look for blankets packed tightly against vents, linen blocking air circulation, items stacked too close to the temperature sensor area, or non-approved items being warmed. Remove excess contents and reload the cabinet with space for airflow. Expected outcome: Cabinet air can circulate normally and temperature should stabilize. If the temperature normalizes after correcting the load, educate staff and document the cause.

steps.title: 6. Check Door Closure and Door Gasket
steps.instructions: A door or gasket issue can cause the heater to run longer than expected or create unstable cabinet temperatures. Inspect the door for proper closure. Check the gasket for tears, flattened sections, gaps, debris, or misalignment. Confirm the door seals evenly. Expected outcome: The cabinet maintains temperature without abnormal heat cycling. If the gasket is damaged or the door does not seal properly, remove the unit from service for repair.

steps.title: 7. Inspect the External Condition of the Warmer
steps.instructions: Look for obvious signs of overheating or damage before continuing. Inspect the warmer for burning smell, discolored cabinet areas, melted plastic, unusual heat around controls, loose power cord, damaged plug, buzzing, or abnormal sounds. Expected outcome: No obvious electrical or heat-damage condition is present. If heat damage, burning odor, or electrical damage is found, stop troubleshooting and send the unit for repair evaluation.

steps.title: 8. Verify the Power Source
steps.instructions: A poor or improper power connection can contribute to abnormal operation. Confirm the warmer is plugged directly into an approved hospital-grade outlet. Avoid extension cords or power strips unless specifically approved by facility policy. Check the outlet for physical damage. If needed, test the outlet with an approved outlet tester. Expected outcome: The warmer is connected to a proper power source. If outlet problems are found, move the warmer to a known-good outlet and notify facilities as appropriate.

steps.title: 9. Compare Display Temperature With an Independent Thermometer
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the displayed temperature matches the actual cabinet temperature. Place an appropriate calibrated thermometer or temperature probe inside the cabinet. Allow enough time for the reading to stabilize. Compare the independent reading with the warmer’s displayed temperature. Expected outcome: If both readings are high, the unit may truly be overheating. If the independent reading is normal but the display reads high, the issue may involve sensing, calibration, or display/control circuitry. If the display is normal but the independent reading is high, the control system may not be regulating properly. If readings do not agree or the cabinet temperature continues rising above setpoint, remove the unit from service.

steps.title: 10. Observe Temperature Recovery After Cooling
steps.instructions: Only perform this step if the unit shows no signs of burning, damage, or unsafe electrical condition. After the warmer cools, plug it into a known-good outlet. Set it to the approved temperature. Monitor the temperature rise. Confirm the heater cycles off and the cabinet stabilizes near the setpoint. Expected outcome: The unit heats normally, stops heating near setpoint, and maintains stable temperature. If the temperature continues climbing beyond setpoint, stop testing and send for repair.

steps.title: 11. Do Not Bypass Temperature Protection
steps.instructions: Do not bypass thermostats, sensors, thermal limits, relays, or control circuits to keep the warmer running. If the unit will not regulate temperature correctly, remove it from service. Expected outcome: The warmer is not returned to patient care with a possible failed control or safety circuit.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported that the Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer temperature was too high and blankets felt hotter than normal.
CCR.Cause: Cabinet temperature exceeded the setpoint during inspection, and external checks found no blocked airflow, incorrect setpoint, or outlet issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the blanket warmer from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation for temperature regulation failure.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual smell, heat, sound, or discoloration

title: Pedigo P-2040 Blanket Warmer - Door Not Closing or Sealing Properly
description: Troubleshooting a Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer door that will not fully close, latch, or maintain a proper seal.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the warmer is not being relied on for immediate patient care. Action: Remove blankets from use if they feel excessively hot, cold, damp, or questionable. If warm blankets are needed, direct staff to another approved blanket warmer. Do not allow staff to force the door closed or continue using the warmer if the door will not seal. Expected outcome: Patient care is not affected by blankets stored in a warmer with an unresolved door problem. Why it matters: A poor door seal can affect temperature stability, cause overheating near the opening, or prevent the warmer from maintaining proper storage conditions.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Door Issue
steps.instructions: Check exactly what staff are reporting. Action: Open and close the door several times. Observe whether the door does not fully close. Observe whether the door springs back open. Observe whether the door closes but does not latch. Observe whether the door has a visible gap. Observe whether the door feels loose or misaligned. Observe whether the door requires excessive force to shut. Expected outcome: The door behavior is confirmed before making adjustments or removing the unit from service. If the door closes normally during inspection, ask staff whether the issue occurs only when the warmer is full.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions Inside the Cabinet
steps.instructions: Inspect the door path and blanket load. Action: Remove or rearrange blankets that may be protruding past the shelf edge. Check for towels, sheets, blanket corners, labels, or packaging caught between the door and cabinet frame. Verify shelves or racks are properly seated and not shifted forward. Expected outcome: The door closes freely without contacting stored items. If removing the obstruction resolves the issue, return the warmer to service after confirming normal door closure and temperature operation.

steps.title: Inspect the Door Gasket or Seal
steps.instructions: Check the gasket around the door for visible damage. Action: Look for flattened gasket sections. Look for torn or missing gasket material. Look for loose gasket edges. Look for hard, brittle, or compressed areas. Look for debris stuck to the gasket. Look for cleaning residue buildup. Wipe the gasket and mating surface with an approved cleaning method if debris is present. Expected outcome: The gasket is clean, flexible, seated correctly, and contacts the cabinet evenly. If a damaged gasket is found, remove the warmer from service until the seal can be replaced or repaired.

steps.title: Check the Door Hinge Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the hinge side for looseness, sagging, or binding. Action: With the door open, gently lift up on the handle side and check for excessive play. Observe whether the door drops, rubs, or shifts out of alignment. Check for loose hinge hardware from the outside only. Do not disassemble the hinge assembly unless authorized and trained to do so. Expected outcome: The door remains square to the cabinet and closes without dragging. If hinge hardware is visibly loose and can be safely tightened externally, tighten it and retest the door. If the hinge is bent, worn, or misaligned, escalate for repair.

steps.title: Inspect the Latch or Catch
steps.instructions: Check whether the latch mechanism engages properly. Action: Observe the latch and strike/catch area while closing the door. Look for latch not lining up with the catch. Look for loose latch hardware. Look for bent strike plate. Look for broken latch component. Look for excessive play in the handle or latch. Expected outcome: The latch aligns with the catch and holds the door closed without force. If the latch is damaged or will not hold, remove the warmer from service and send it for repair.

steps.title: Check for Cabinet or Door Frame Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the warmer for signs of impact or physical damage. Action: Look for dents, bent frame edges, twisted door alignment, or damage from carts, beds, or transport. Check whether the warmer sits level and stable on the floor or counter. Confirm the cabinet is not leaning, rocking, or placed on an uneven surface. Expected outcome: The cabinet is square, stable, and not physically preventing the door from sealing. If the frame or door is bent, do not attempt to force alignment in the clinical area. Remove the unit from service for bench evaluation or vendor repair.

steps.title: Verify Temperature Stability After Door Closure
steps.instructions: If the door issue is corrected, confirm the warmer operates normally. Action: Close the door fully. Allow the warmer to stabilize. Check that the temperature display or indicator behavior is normal. Confirm the door remains closed during normal operation. Expected outcome: The warmer maintains normal operation with the door fully sealed. If the door closes but temperature recovery seems abnormal, continue troubleshooting as a temperature-control issue.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer door would not close completely and appeared to have a gap at the seal.
CCR.Cause: Found blanket material protruding past the shelf edge and preventing the door gasket from contacting the cabinet frame.
CCR.Resolution: Repositioned blanket load, cleaned the gasket contact area, verified the door closed and latched properly, and confirmed normal warmer operation.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested, if power or heat complaints are also reported
Door closes with normal blanket load

title: Pedigo P-2040 Blanket Warmer - Blankets Not Warming Evenly
description: Troubleshooting uneven blanket warming caused by loading, airflow restriction, door seal issues, settings, location, or warmer performance problems.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not place blankets into patient use if they are excessively hot, cold in sections, damp, or warmed inconsistently. Action: Remove questionable blankets from use. Confirm staff have access to properly warmed blankets from another warmer if patient care requires them. Allow the warmer to stabilize before returning it to service. Expected outcome: Patients are not exposed to blankets with unsafe or inconsistent temperatures.

steps.title: Verify the Complaint
steps.instructions: Confirm what staff mean by “not warming evenly.” Action: Ask whether the issue involves blankets cold near the door. Ask whether the issue involves blankets warm on top but cool underneath. Ask whether the issue involves one side of the cabinet warmer than the other. Ask whether the issue involves slow warming after restocking. Ask whether the issue involves random temperature inconsistency. Check the displayed or set temperature if the unit has a temperature display or control. Expected outcome: The failure pattern is understood before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check for Overloading
steps.instructions: Inspect how the blankets are loaded inside the cabinet. Action: Make sure blankets are not packed tightly against the sides, rear, door, or shelves. Confirm there is space for warm air to circulate around the load. Remove excess blankets and retest after allowing time for the cabinet to recover. Expected outcome: Blankets warm more evenly when airflow is not restricted. Why it matters: Blanket warmers rely on heat circulation. A fully packed cabinet can create cold spots.

steps.title: Check Blanket Placement
steps.instructions: Look for blankets stacked in a way that blocks heat movement. Action: Separate dense stacks. Avoid placing heavy folded bundles directly against temperature sensors, vents, rear panels, or heater areas. Reload the warmer with smaller, looser stacks. Expected outcome: Heat distributes more evenly throughout the cabinet.

steps.title: Check Door Closure and Gasket Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect the door and gasket. Action: Confirm the door closes fully and latches properly. Look for torn, compressed, dirty, loose, or hardened gasket areas. Check whether the door is being left open frequently. Expected outcome: The cabinet retains heat properly and does not lose heat near the door. If the gasket is damaged or the door does not seal, remove the warmer from service for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Check for Airflow Blockage Inside the Cabinet
steps.instructions: Inspect the interior for blocked vents, liners, shelves, or misplaced items. Action: Remove non-approved items. Check for blankets, packaging, or debris blocking air openings. Confirm shelves are seated correctly and not obstructing heat movement. Expected outcome: Warm air can circulate normally inside the warmer.

steps.title: Check Temperature Setting
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer is set appropriately for blanket warming per facility policy. Action: Confirm the set temperature has not been lowered accidentally. Compare the setting to hospital policy for blanket warmers. Do not raise the temperature beyond approved limits to compensate for poor warming. Expected outcome: The warmer is operating at the intended temperature range.

steps.title: Allow Recovery Time After Restocking
steps.instructions: Determine whether the issue happens immediately after new blankets are added. Action: Ask staff when the warmer was last restocked. Allow the warmer enough time to recover after a large load is added. Recheck blanket temperature after the cabinet stabilizes. Expected outcome: Recently loaded blankets may warm normally once enough recovery time has passed.

steps.title: Check Location and Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Inspect the warmer’s location. Action: Confirm the warmer is not near a cold air return, exterior door, draft, HVAC vent, or unconditioned area. Make sure the unit has proper clearance around it for ventilation. Expected outcome: Room conditions are not causing temperature instability or uneven heat loss.

steps.title: Check Power Stability
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer has stable power. Action: Confirm the power cord is fully seated and not damaged. Verify the outlet is working properly. Check whether the plug, cord, or outlet feels unusually warm. Do not use extension cords or power strips unless specifically approved by facility policy and manufacturer guidance. Expected outcome: The warmer receives stable power without unsafe electrical conditions.

steps.title: Check for Abnormal Signs
steps.instructions: Inspect the warmer during operation. Action: Listen for unusual noises. Check for unusual heat, burning smell, discoloration, or signs of overheating. Look for error indicators, abnormal display behavior, or temperature swings. Expected outcome: No obvious safety or performance abnormality is present. If there is odor, overheating, electrical damage, or abnormal operation, remove the warmer from service immediately.

steps.title: Perform a Temperature Consistency Check
steps.instructions: Use an approved thermometer or temperature logging method if available. Action: Check temperature in multiple safe locations inside the warmer after stabilization. Compare top, middle, bottom, front, and rear areas. Document whether one area is consistently cooler or hotter. Expected outcome: Clinical Engineering can determine whether this is a loading/airflow issue or a true warmer performance issue. If uneven temperatures remain after proper loading, door seal checks, and stabilization, stop troubleshooting and escalate.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported that the Pedigo P-2040 blanket warmer was heating blankets unevenly, with some blankets warm and others still cool.
CCR.Cause: Warmer was found overloaded with tightly packed blankets restricting internal heat circulation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed excess blankets, reloaded cabinet with proper spacing, allowed warmer to stabilize, and verified improved blanket warming before returning to service.

title: Pedigo P-2055 Blanket Warmer - Not Heating
description: Troubleshooting a Pedigo P-2055 blanket warmer that powers on but does not warm, heats slowly, or fails to maintain temperature.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not rely on a blanket warmer that is not heating correctly for patient warming needs. Remove the blanket warmer from clinical use if staff report cold blankets, inconsistent warming, overheating, burning smell, or abnormal operation. Check whether warmed blankets are needed for active patient care. Provide another approved blanket warmer if needed. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without relying on equipment that may not maintain safe warming temperatures. Why it matters: Blanket warmers are not life-support devices, but incorrect warming can affect patient comfort, infection control workflows, and burn-prevention expectations.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm what “not heating” means before assuming a heater failure. Ask staff whether the unit is not powering on. Ask staff whether the unit is powered on but staying cold. Ask staff whether the unit is heating slowly. Ask staff whether the unit is reaching temperature and then cooling down. Ask staff whether the unit is displaying an alarm or abnormal temperature reading. Expected outcome: The failure mode is clearly identified. If the warmer has no power at all, troubleshoot it as a power issue instead of a heating issue.

steps.title: Check That the Unit Is Plugged Into a Working Outlet
steps.instructions: Verify basic power before checking the warmer. Confirm the power cord is fully inserted into the wall outlet. Check the wall outlet with an outlet tester or another known-working device. Avoid using extension cords or non-approved power strips. Expected outcome: The outlet supplies proper power and the warmer remains powered. If the outlet is dead, relocate the warmer to a working outlet or notify Facilities. If this resolves the issue, return the warmer to service after confirming normal heating.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and Plug
steps.instructions: A damaged cord can allow the unit to appear powered but still cause unreliable operation. Inspect the power cord for cuts, exposed conductors, flattened sections, loose strain relief, or heat damage. Check the plug blades for looseness, discoloration, or signs of overheating. Expected outcome: The cord and plug are intact with no visible damage. If damage is found, remove the unit from service and send it for repair.

steps.title: Confirm the Power Switch and Controls Are Set Correctly
steps.instructions: Incorrect settings can look like a heating failure. Confirm the warmer is turned on. Check the temperature setpoint. Make sure the control is not set unusually low. Verify staff did not recently adjust the temperature or leave the unit off after cleaning. Expected outcome: The unit is powered on and set to an appropriate warming temperature. If the setpoint was incorrect and the unit begins heating normally, document the finding and return the unit to service.

steps.title: Allow Adequate Warm-Up Time
steps.instructions: Blanket warmers may take time to recover temperature, especially when recently loaded or opened repeatedly. Start with an empty or lightly loaded warmer. Close the door fully. Allow the unit time to warm without repeated door openings. Observe whether the temperature rises gradually. Expected outcome: The displayed or measured temperature should increase over time. If the unit heats normally after adequate warm-up, no repair may be needed.

steps.title: Check Door Closure and Door Gasket Condition
steps.instructions: A door that does not seal can prevent the cabinet from reaching temperature. Confirm the door closes fully. Inspect the gasket for tears, gaps, deformation, or debris. Check for blankets blocking the door from sealing. Expected outcome: The door seals evenly and does not leak excessive heat. If the door was blocked or the gasket was dirty, correct the issue and retest. If the gasket is damaged or the door does not close properly, remove the warmer from service for repair.

steps.title: Check for Overloading or Poor Air Circulation
steps.instructions: Too many blankets can block heat circulation and make the warmer appear weak. Look inside the cabinet. Confirm blankets are not packed tightly against vents, shelves, or internal airflow areas. Reduce the load if the warmer is overfilled. Expected outcome: Air can circulate around the blankets and through the cabinet. If reducing the load restores normal heating, educate staff and return the unit to service.

steps.title: Verify the Temperature With an Independent Thermometer
steps.instructions: Do not rely only on staff perception of blanket warmth. Place a calibrated thermometer or temperature logger inside the warmer. Allow the temperature to stabilize. Compare the independent reading to the warmer’s display or setpoint. Expected outcome: The cabinet temperature should reasonably match the setpoint and displayed temperature. If the display shows heating but the independent thermometer shows low temperature, suspect a heating, sensor, control, or calibration issue.

steps.title: Listen and Observe for Abnormal Operation
steps.instructions: External signs can help determine whether the issue is simple or repair-related. Observe the unit during a heating attempt. Check for no heat output. Check for clicking relays without heating. Check for unusual fan noise, if equipped. Check for burning smell. Check for excessive heat at the plug or cord. Check for temperature display that does not change. Expected outcome: The unit operates normally with no unusual smell, heat, or sounds. If there is a burning smell, abnormal heat, or electrical concern, unplug the unit and remove it from service immediately.

steps.title: Perform a Basic Functional Check
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the unit can heat and hold temperature safely. With the unit empty or lightly loaded, power it on. Set it to the normal facility-approved blanket warming range. Allow the cabinet to warm. Confirm the temperature rises and stabilizes. Expected outcome: The warmer reaches and maintains temperature without alarms, abnormal odor, or erratic control behavior. If it passes, return to service per department policy. If it fails, escalate for repair.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Pedigo P-2055 blanket warmer was powered on but blankets remained cold.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet power and correct temperature setting; unit failed to raise cabinet temperature during functional testing.
CCR.Resolution: Removed blanket warmer from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation for suspected internal heating circuit failure.

helpfulDetails: Whether vents or airflow paths were blocked
Unusual smell, sound, heat, or indicator behavior

title: Pedigo P-2055 Blanket Warmer - Temperature Too Low
description: Troubleshooting low blanket warmer temperature caused by power, settings, door seal, loading, airflow, or heating performance issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not use blankets from the warmer if staff report they are not reaching the expected temperature. Action: Remove the blanket warmer from active clinical use if warmed blankets are needed for patient care and the unit cannot maintain temperature. Provide blankets from another approved warmer if available. Confirm staff are not compensating by increasing settings beyond facility policy. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without relying on a warmer that may not be maintaining proper temperature. Why it matters: Under-warmed blankets may affect patient comfort and thermal support, while improper setting changes can create burn or overheating risk.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Temperature Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the issue is an actual low-temperature condition or a misunderstanding of normal warm-up behavior. Action: Ask staff what temperature was expected, what temperature was observed, and how long the unit had been warming. Check the displayed temperature or temperature indicator. Compare the set temperature to the actual chamber temperature if the unit displays both. Expected outcome: You confirm whether the warmer is truly below the set temperature. If the unit is still warming up after being loaded, recently powered on, or frequently opened, allow reasonable recovery time and recheck. If the temperature recovers normally, return the unit to service and stop.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Cord Connection
steps.instructions: Verify that the warmer has stable AC power. Action: Confirm the power cord is fully seated and not damaged. Check that the outlet is live. Verify the unit is not plugged into a switched outlet that may have been turned off. Inspect for loose plugs, damaged prongs, overheated connectors, or signs of arcing. Expected outcome: The unit remains powered without flickering, resets, or intermittent shutdowns. If power is restored and the warmer returns to the correct temperature, document the cause and stop.

steps.title: Confirm the Temperature Setting
steps.instructions: Check that the set temperature is appropriate and has not been lowered. Action: Review the temperature control setting. Confirm it matches the department’s approved blanket warming range. Do not exceed facility policy or manufacturer temperature limits. Expected outcome: The warmer is set correctly for blanket warming. If the setting was too low, correct it, allow the warmer to stabilize, verify temperature recovery, and stop.

steps.title: Inspect Door Closure and Gasket Seal
steps.instructions: A leaking door seal can prevent the chamber from reaching or maintaining temperature. Action: Check that the door closes fully. Inspect the gasket for cracks, gaps, compression damage, debris, or sections pulling away. Confirm blankets or packaging are not blocking the door from sealing. Expected outcome: The door closes evenly and the gasket makes full contact. If a blocked door or debris issue is corrected and temperature recovers, return the unit to service and stop.

steps.title: Check Blanket Loading and Air Circulation
steps.instructions: Overloading can block airflow and cause slow or uneven heating. Action: Inspect how blankets are loaded. Remove excessive blankets if the chamber is packed tightly. Make sure blankets are not blocking vents, air channels, sensors, or circulation paths. Expected outcome: Air can circulate through the chamber and around stored blankets. If reducing the load allows the warmer to recover to set temperature, educate staff on proper loading and stop.

steps.title: Check for Frequent Door Opening
steps.instructions: Frequent access can make the warmer appear weak even when it is functioning normally. Action: Ask staff whether the door has been opened repeatedly. Observe whether the door is left open during stocking or blanket removal. Allow the unit to recover with the door closed. Expected outcome: Temperature rises back toward the set point once the door remains closed. If the warmer recovers normally, document environmental/use-related cause and stop.

steps.title: Inspect for External Airflow or Room Conditions
steps.instructions: Cold drafts or poor placement can affect temperature recovery. Action: Check whether the warmer is near an air vent, doorway, exterior wall, or high-traffic area. Confirm the unit has proper clearance around it. Make sure vents are not blocked by carts, walls, supplies, or dust buildup. Expected outcome: The warmer has adequate ventilation and is not exposed to excessive cooling airflow. If correcting placement or blocked ventilation resolves the issue, return the unit to service and stop.

steps.title: Listen for Abnormal Operation
steps.instructions: A low-temperature complaint may be related to heater, fan, relay, or thermostat performance. Action: Listen for unusual clicking, buzzing, fan noise changes, or silence when heat should be active. Check for unusual heat, burning smell, discoloration, or signs of overheating. Do not continue troubleshooting if there is smoke, odor, excessive heat, or electrical damage. Expected outcome: The unit operates without abnormal sounds, smells, or visible damage. If unsafe signs are present, remove the unit from service immediately.

steps.title: Verify Temperature with an Approved Method
steps.instructions: If the warmer appears functional but staff still report low temperature, confirm with an appropriate thermometer or facility-approved temperature verification method. Action: Allow the unit to stabilize with the door closed. Measure chamber temperature according to department practice. Compare the reading to the set temperature and acceptable tolerance. Expected outcome: The measured chamber temperature agrees with the set temperature within an acceptable range. If the temperature is correct, document verification and return the unit to service. If the temperature remains low, remove the unit from service for bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported the Pedigo P-2055 blanket warmer was not heating blankets adequately and chamber temperature appeared too low.
CCR.Cause: Checked power, set temperature, door seal, loading, airflow, and recovery time; unit remained below expected temperature after stabilization.
CCR.Resolution: Removed the Pedigo P-2055 from service, labeled it Out of Service, and sent it for bench evaluation of heating and temperature control components.

title: Pedigo P-2055 Blanket Warmer - Temperature Too High
description: Troubleshooting high temperature complaints, overheating, incorrect setpoint, blocked airflow, sensor concerns, or unsafe blanket warmer heat conditions.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not continue using the blanket warmer if staff report unusually hot blankets, excessive cabinet heat, burning smell, or a high-temperature alarm. Action: Remove blankets from patient use until the temperature concern is verified. If blankets feel excessively hot, notify clinical staff not to place them on patients. Expected outcome: Patient exposure to overheated blankets is prevented. Why it matters: Overheated blankets can cause patient burns, especially for sedated, elderly, neonatal, or low-sensation patients.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Temperature Issue
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the unit is actually overheating or if the complaint is related to setpoint expectations. Action: Check the displayed temperature, set temperature, actual cabinet temperature, and any alarm or warning behavior. Ask staff whether the issue was constant or intermittent. Expected outcome: You know whether the warmer is above setpoint, heating continuously, or simply set higher than expected. If the issue is explained by an incorrect setpoint, correct the setpoint, monitor operation, document findings, and stop.

steps.title: Check the Temperature Setpoint
steps.instructions: Verify the warmer is not set above the department’s approved temperature range. Action: Review the current temperature setting and compare it with facility policy for blanket warming. Expected outcome: The setpoint is appropriate for safe clinical use. If the warmer was set too high, lower it to the approved range, allow the cabinet to stabilize, recheck temperature, document the user-setting issue, and stop.

steps.title: Inspect for Overloading or Blocked Airflow
steps.instructions: Overpacking the cabinet can trap heat and create uneven or excessive blanket temperatures. Action: Open the warmer and inspect how blankets are loaded. Check for items blocking vents, covering internal airflow paths, or packed tightly against the temperature sensing area. Expected outcome: Blankets are loaded loosely enough for air circulation, and vents are not obstructed. If airflow obstruction caused the issue, correct the loading condition, allow the warmer to stabilize, recheck temperature, document findings, and stop.

steps.title: Confirm Only Approved Items Are Inside
steps.instructions: Blanket warmers should not be used to warm fluids, unauthorized supplies, plastic-wrapped items, or equipment unless specifically approved. Action: Inspect the cabinet contents. Remove any non-approved items and ask staff what had been stored in the warmer when the problem occurred. Expected outcome: The cabinet contains only approved warming items. If inappropriate contents caused the high-temperature complaint, remove them, notify the department, document the finding, and stop.

steps.title: Check Door Seal and Door Closure
steps.instructions: A damaged gasket or door that does not close properly can cause unstable heating behavior and poor temperature control. Action: Inspect the door gasket, latch, hinges, and door alignment. Confirm the door closes fully and seals evenly. Expected outcome: The door seals correctly with no obvious gaps, damage, or misalignment. If the door does not seal correctly, remove the unit from service for repair evaluation.

steps.title: Inspect the Area Around the Warmer
steps.instructions: Poor ventilation around the unit can contribute to excess heat buildup. Action: Check that the warmer has adequate clearance around vents and is not placed near external heat sources, direct sunlight, covered vents, or restricted airflow. Expected outcome: The unit has proper surrounding ventilation and is not exposed to external heat sources. If the location is causing heat buildup, correct the placement if allowed, monitor temperature, document the environmental cause, and stop.

steps.title: Measure Cabinet Temperature Independently
steps.instructions: Do not rely only on the display if the complaint is overheating. Action: Use a calibrated thermometer or temperature logger appropriate for equipment checks. Place it in the cabinet and allow enough time for the reading to stabilize. Expected outcome: Independent temperature measurement is close to the warmer’s displayed temperature and within the approved operating range. If the independent reading confirms excessive temperature, remove the unit from service.

steps.title: Check for Continuous Heating Behavior
steps.instructions: A warmer that continues heating past setpoint may have a control, relay, thermostat, or sensor issue. Action: Observe whether the heater cycles normally or appears to continue heating even after the cabinet reaches setpoint. Expected outcome: Heating cycles off or reduces appropriately as the unit reaches set temperature. If heating appears uncontrolled, remove the unit from service and send for repair or bench evaluation.

steps.title: Inspect for Obvious Damage, Smell, or Heat Stress
steps.instructions: Look for external signs that the unit may be unsafe. Action: Check the power cord, plug, cabinet surfaces, controls, display, vents, and interior for discoloration, unusual smell, abnormal heat, melted material, or signs of electrical damage. Expected outcome: No obvious physical damage, burning smell, unusual heat, or unsafe condition is present. If any damage, burning smell, or abnormal heat is found, unplug the warmer if safe to do so, label it Out of Service, and escalate for repair.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported the Pedigo P-2055 blanket warmer was running too hot and blankets felt hotter than normal.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found the cabinet was overloaded with blankets packed against the internal airflow path, causing poor heat circulation and elevated blanket temperature.
CCR.Resolution: Removed excess blankets, restored proper airflow, verified cabinet temperature with an independent thermometer, and returned the warmer to service after temperature stabilized within the approved range.

helpfulDetails: Any unusual smell, heat, discoloration, or melted material
Whether blankets were removed from patient use
Environmental factors, including nearby heat sources, direct sunlight, or restricted airflow
Power behavior and whether the heater appeared to run continuously
Indicator lights, display behavior, or warning messages observed

title: Pedigo P-2055 Blanket Warmer - Door Not Closing or Sealing Properly
description: Troubleshooting poor door closure, weak sealing, air leakage, gasket damage, or latch/hinge alignment issues on a Pedigo P-2055 blanket warmer.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot the warmer while it is actively needed for patient care. Remove patient blankets from the affected chamber if door closure is unreliable. Use another blanket warmer if warmed blankets are needed immediately. Allow the unit to cool before inspecting the door, gasket, hinge area, or interior surfaces. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without relying on a warmer that may not maintain proper cabinet temperature. Why it matters: A door that does not seal properly can cause heat loss, inaccurate warming, longer warm-up times, or overheating compensation concerns.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Door Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm exactly what staff are seeing. Open and close the affected door several times. Check whether the issue is: Door will not fully close. Door closes but pops open. Door closes but has a visible gap. Door gasket does not contact the cabinet evenly. Door rubs against the frame. Latch does not catch. Glass door or handle feels loose. Expected outcome: The door issue is confirmed and narrowed to closure, sealing, latch, gasket, or alignment behavior. If the door closes and seals normally during testing, document “unable to duplicate” and return to service only after confirming stable temperature operation.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions Inside the Cabinet
steps.instructions: Look for simple causes before assuming hardware failure. Inspect the blanket load. Make sure blankets are not protruding past the shelf or pressing against the door. Verify the shelf or blanket support assembly is seated correctly. Remove excess blankets and close the door again. Expected outcome: The door closes without pressure from blankets or internal components. If removing or repositioning blankets resolves the issue, return the warmer to service and educate staff not to overfill the chamber.

steps.title: Inspect the Door Gasket
steps.instructions: Check the gasket condition and seating. Inspect the full perimeter of the door gasket. Look for: Tears. Holes. Flattened areas. Loose sections. Missing gasket material. Debris stuck to the gasket. Gasket pulled away from the door. Pedigo’s operator guidance specifically calls for checking the door gasket for tears, holes, firm attachment, and proper sealing when the door is closed. Expected outcome: The gasket is intact, clean, attached, and making even contact with the cabinet. If the gasket is damaged, loose, or no longer seals, remove the unit from service or the affected chamber from use and escalate for gasket replacement or repair.

steps.title: Clean the Gasket and Door Contact Surface
steps.instructions: Debris can prevent a good seal. Disconnect power if cleaning around the door, gasket, or cabinet edge. Allow the warmer to cool. Wipe the door gasket and cabinet mating surface with an approved mild cleaning method. Remove lint, tape, blanket fibers, adhesive residue, or dried cleaning product. Dry the gasket and door contact surface fully. Expected outcome: The door closes cleanly and the gasket contacts the cabinet without obstruction. Why it matters: Pedigo care guidance notes that the cabinet and door gasket should be kept free of debris, and door gaskets should be wiped thoroughly because debris can accumulate there. If cleaning resolves the issue, return the unit to service after confirming the door remains closed and temperature operation is normal.

steps.title: Check the Door Handle and Latch Area
steps.instructions: Verify the latch hardware is not loose, bent, or blocked. Inspect the handle and latch area. Check whether the latch catches normally when the door is closed. Look for loose screws, damaged latch components, bent strike hardware, or foreign material preventing engagement. Do not force the door closed. Expected outcome: The latch engages smoothly and holds the door closed without excessive force. If the latch does not engage or the hardware appears damaged, remove the warmer from service and send for repair.

steps.title: Inspect Door Alignment and Hinge Condition
steps.instructions: A misaligned door can cause poor sealing even when the gasket is good. Stand in front of the warmer and compare the door spacing around the frame. Check whether the door sags, rubs, or sits unevenly. Gently lift the door handle while closing to see if sagging changes the seal. Inspect hinge areas for looseness, damage, or shifted hardware. Expected outcome: The door sits squarely in the frame and seals evenly. If the door is sagging, binding, or visibly misaligned, remove the device from service and escalate for hinge or door adjustment/repair.

steps.title: Check Casters, Leveling, and Cabinet Position
steps.instructions: Cabinet twist can affect door closure. Confirm the warmer is on a stable, level surface. Check that casters or fixed legs are secure. Make sure the unit is not wedged against a wall, cart, cabinet, or other equipment. If safe, reposition the warmer and test the door again. Expected outcome: The cabinet sits stable and the door closes without frame distortion. If repositioning or stabilizing the warmer resolves the issue, return it to service and document the environmental cause.

steps.title: Check for Door or Frame Damage
steps.instructions: Look for signs of impact or transport damage. Inspect the door frame, cabinet front, glass window, hinges, handle, and latch area. Look for dents, bent stainless steel, cracked glass, loose trim, or evidence the door was struck. Expected outcome: No structural damage is found. If the door, glass, hinge, latch, or cabinet frame is damaged, remove the unit from service and send it for repair.

steps.title: Verify Temperature Stability After Door Correction
steps.instructions: If the door issue is corrected, confirm the warmer is operating normally before returning it to use. Close the door and allow the warmer to operate normally. Verify the door remains closed. Confirm the displayed temperature stabilizes appropriately. Check that no unusual heat, smell, noise, or alarm behavior is present. Expected outcome: Door closure is reliable and the warmer maintains normal operation. If the door opens on its own, temperature does not recover, or abnormal conditions are present, remove the unit from service.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported that the Pedigo P-2055 blanket warmer door would not close completely and appeared to be leaking warm air.
CCR.Cause: Inspection found blanket fibers and debris on the door gasket preventing full gasket contact with the cabinet frame.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned the gasket and cabinet sealing surface, verified the door closed properly, and confirmed normal temperature recovery before returning the warmer to service.

helpfulDetails: Outlet tested, if power was disconnected or unit was moved
Affected chamber, if only one door has the issue
Door closes fully or remains partially open
Any unusual heat, smell, or noise

title: Pedigo P-2055 Blanket Warmer - Blankets Not Warming Evenly
description: Troubleshooting uneven blanket warming caused by loading, airflow, door sealing, settings, or temperature-control issues.

steps.title: 1. Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Do not place cold, overheated, or unevenly warmed blankets into patient use until the warmer is verified. Action: Remove questionable blankets from service. Check for blankets that feel excessively hot, cold, damp, or unevenly warmed. Use blankets from another approved warmer if patient care requires immediate use. Expected outcome: Patient care continues without using blankets from a warmer that may not be heating consistently. Why it matters: Uneven blanket warming can lead to patient discomfort, delayed care, or unsafe heat exposure if some blankets are much warmer than others.

steps.title: 2. Verify the Reported Problem
steps.instructions: Confirm whether the issue is truly uneven warming or a loading/use issue. Action: Ask clinical staff: Was the warmer recently restocked? Were blankets added cold or damp? Are only the front blankets warm? Are the top, bottom, or rear blankets staying cooler? Has the door been opened frequently? Expected outcome: You identify whether the problem follows a specific shelf, area, or usage pattern. If the issue is explained by recent restocking or frequent door opening, allow the warmer time to recover and recheck.

steps.title: 3. Check the Temperature Setting
steps.instructions: Verify that the warmer is set within the facility-approved blanket warming range. Action: Check the displayed temperature or thermostat setting. Confirm the setting has not been turned down, changed accidentally, or left below the expected range. Do not raise the temperature above facility policy or manufacturer limits to compensate for poor warming. Expected outcome: The warmer is set appropriately and is not being operated outside safe limits. If the setting was incorrect, correct it, allow the unit to stabilize, and recheck blanket temperature.

steps.title: 4. Inspect Blanket Loading
steps.instructions: Check whether the cabinet is packed too tightly. Action: Open the warmer and inspect how blankets are stacked. Look for: Overfilled cabinet. Blankets pressed tightly against walls. Blankets blocking vents or internal air paths. Heavy stacks preventing heat movement. Blankets loaded unevenly on one side. Expected outcome: Air and heat can circulate around the blankets. If overloading is found, reduce the blanket load, restack loosely, allow recovery time, and recheck.

steps.title: 5. Check for Blocked Airflow or Heat Paths
steps.instructions: Uneven warming often happens when airflow or heat distribution is blocked. Action: Inspect the inside of the cabinet for: Blankets covering vents. Liners, bags, or packaging left inside. Items other than blankets stored in the warmer. Debris near internal openings. Expected outcome: Internal airflow and heat transfer areas are clear. If blocked, remove the obstruction, reload properly, and allow the warmer to stabilize.

steps.title: 6. Inspect the Door Seal and Door Closure
steps.instructions: A poor door seal can allow heat loss, especially near the front of the warmer. Action: Check that the door closes fully. Inspect the gasket for: Tears. Flattened areas. Loose sections. Gaps. Debris preventing proper sealing. Expected outcome: The door seals evenly around the cabinet. If the door does not seal correctly, remove the warmer from service if temperature cannot be maintained consistently.

steps.title: 7. Check Door Opening Frequency
steps.instructions: Frequent door opening can make the front or upper blankets feel cooler than the rest of the cabinet. Action: Ask staff how often the warmer is accessed. Observe whether the door is being left open during restocking or patient care. Expected outcome: The warmer has enough closed-door recovery time to maintain stable cabinet temperature. If usage pattern is the cause, educate staff to minimize door-open time and avoid leaving the door ajar.

steps.title: 8. Allow Recovery After Restocking
steps.instructions: Cold blankets can take time to warm evenly, especially after a large restock. Action: Confirm when the warmer was last loaded. If newly loaded, allow adequate warm-up time with the door closed. Recheck blankets from different areas after the recovery period. Expected outcome: Blanket temperatures become more consistent after the cabinet stabilizes. If uneven warming remains after adequate recovery time, continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: 9. Compare Actual Temperature in Different Cabinet Areas
steps.instructions: Use an approved temperature check method if available. Action: Check temperature in several areas of the cabinet, such as: Top shelf. Bottom shelf. Front area. Rear area. Left and right sides. Compare findings to the displayed temperature or expected operating range. Expected outcome: Cabinet temperature is reasonably consistent throughout the warmer. If one area is significantly cooler or hotter, suspect airflow, door seal, heater, sensor, or control issue.

steps.title: 10. Check for Abnormal Signs
steps.instructions: Inspect for signs that the warmer may have an internal heating or control problem. Action: Look and listen for: No heat production. Unusual clicking. Burning smell. Excessive heat. Discolored interior surfaces. Damaged power cord. Loose plug. Intermittent display or indicator lights. Expected outcome: No unsafe electrical, overheating, or control symptoms are present. If unusual heat, smell, damaged wiring, or erratic operation is found, remove the warmer from service immediately.

steps.title: 11. Power Cycle Only if Appropriate
steps.instructions: If the warmer has a display or control issue, a controlled reset may help confirm behavior. Action: Remove blankets if needed for safe evaluation. Turn the warmer off. Wait briefly. Turn the unit back on. Observe whether the display, indicators, and heating behavior return normally. Expected outcome: The warmer restarts normally and begins heating consistently. If the issue returns or the warmer does not recover properly, stop troubleshooting and send for bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Clinical staff reported that blankets in the Pedigo P-2055 were not warming evenly, with some blankets remaining cooler than others.
CCR.Cause: Cabinet was found overfilled with blankets packed tightly against the internal heat circulation areas, limiting even heat distribution.
CCR.Resolution: Reduced blanket load, restacked blankets to allow airflow, verified door closure, allowed recovery time, and confirmed improved warming before returning unit to service.

helpfulDetails: Actual temperature readings from different cabinet areas
Whether vents or airflow paths were blocked
Any unusual smell, heat, sound, or indicator behavior
